text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. When developers refactor, they clean up messy code by breaking large functions into smaller ones, renaming unclear variables, and removing duplicate logic. This makes the code more readable and easier to maintain. Many teams practice refactoring regularly to reduce technical debt, which is the accumulated cost of quick fixes and poor design choices. By refactoring early and often, developers save time and effort in the long run, as clean code is simpler to test, debug, and extend with new features.
